The concept described below, which can be found in existing literature such as  Ovchinnikov (2002) , possesses distinct geometric characteristics and plays a crucial role in the subsequent characterization of the property of well-gradedness (see  Theorem 3.1 ).

Definition 2.1 
                            Let  
                                  K 
                                be a non-empty family of mappings of  
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               , and  
                                  
                                     
                                        { 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        } 
                                     
                                     ⊆ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               . We say that  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                                are  adjacent  in  
                                  K 
                                if the interval  
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                in  
                                  K 
                                contains only the elements  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                               , i.e.,  
                                  
                                     
                                        [ 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        ] 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                                in  
                                  K 
                               .

Let  
                                  K 
                                be a non-empty family of mappings of  
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               , and  
                                  
                                     
                                        { 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        } 
                                     
                                     ⊆ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               . We say that  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                                are  adjacent  in  
                                  K 
                                if the interval  
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                in  
                                  K 
                                contains only the elements  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                               , i.e.,  
                                  
                                     
                                        [ 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        ] 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                                in  
                                  K 
                               .

It is evident that two elements  
                            K 
                          and  
                            L 
                          in  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                          are adjacent if and only if their distance is 1. However, it is important to note that when the family  
                            K 
                          is not equal to  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                         , the adjacency of elements  
                            K 
                          and  
                            L 
                          within  
                            K 
                          does not necessarily imply a distance of 1, i.e.,  
                            
                               d 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ) 
                               
                               = 
                               1 
                            
                         . A counterexample is provided in  Example 2.2 , where  
                            
                               d 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  210 
                                  , 
                                  120 
                                  ) 
                               
                               = 
                               2 
                            
                          and  
                            
                               d 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  000 
                                  , 
                                  120 
                                  ) 
                               
                               = 
                               3 
                            
                         , despite  
                            
                               { 
                               210 
                               , 
                               120 
                               } 
                            
                          and  
                            
                               { 
                               000 
                               , 
                               120 
                               } 
                            
                          being adjacent in  
                            K 
                         .

The concept of a polytomous knowledge structure may have varied in previous literature ( Heller, 2021; Schrepp, 1997; Stefanutti et al., 2020, 2023 ). In this context, we will adhere to the definition provided by  Heller (2021) .

Definition 2.2 
                            Let  
                                  K 
                                be a non-empty family of elements of  
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               . Then  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                is defined as a  polytomous (knowledge) structure  on  
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                if  
                                  K 
                                contains the elements  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        ⊤ 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        ⊥ 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                               , and  
                                  
                                     ⋃ 
                                     K 
                                     = 
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               . The elements  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                                are referred to as  polytomous (knowledge) states .

Let  
                                  K 
                                be a non-empty family of elements of  
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               . Then  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                is defined as a  polytomous (knowledge) structure  on  
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                if  
                                  K 
                                contains the elements  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        ⊤ 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        ⊥ 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                               , and  
                                  
                                     ⋃ 
                                     K 
                                     = 
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               . The elements  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                                are referred to as  polytomous (knowledge) states .

If we consider the polytomous state  
                            K 
                          as a set within  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               V 
                            
                         , then  
                            
                               K 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  p 
                                  ) 
                               
                               = 
                               v 
                            
                          implies that the pair  
                            
                               ( 
                               p 
                               , 
                               v 
                               ) 
                            
                          belongs to the state  
                            K 
                         . To simplify future discussions, we will adopt the notation proposed by  Heller (2021) , where  
                            
                               ( 
                               p 
                               , 
                               v 
                               ) 
                            
                          is represented as  
                            
                               
                                  p 
                               
                               
                                  v 
                               
                            
                          and referred to as a virtual item. In this notation,  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               ∈ 
                               K 
                            
                          indicates that for the item  
                            p 
                         , it can be resolved up to level  
                            v 
                          within the polytomous state  
                            K 
                         .

To extend the notion of polytomous states,  Heller (2021)  introduced an  extended polytomous state  
                         
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                          for a polytomous state  
                            K 
                         , defined as:  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               
                                  
                                     ⋃ 
                                  
                                  
                                     p 
                                     ∈ 
                                     Q 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        u 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∣ 
                                  u 
                                  ≤ 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         . In this extended state  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                         , a virtual item  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               ∈ 
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                            
                          represents the requirement that the corresponding real response value  
                            
                               K 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  p 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                          for an individual on item  
                            p 
                          must be greater than or equal to level  
                            v 
                         , denoted as  
                            
                               v 
                               ≤ 
                               K 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  p 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                         . Furthermore, in the context of a polytomous knowledge structure  
                            K 
                         , the  extended polytomous knowledge structure  
                         
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                          is defined as  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∣ 
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         . To facilitate discussions, the following notations will also be used:  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        K 
                                        ∈ 
                                        K 
                                        ∣ 
                                        
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                        
                                        ∈ 
                                        K 
                                        } 
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                        ∈ 
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                        ∣ 
                                        
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                        
                                        ∈ 
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                        } 
                                     
                                     . 
                                  
                               
                            
                         For a comprehensive understanding of the extended polytomous knowledge state and structure, please refer to  Heller (2021) .

Stefanutti et al. (2020)  extended the concepts of  
                            ∪ 
                         -closed and  
                            ∩ 
                         -closed from KST to polytomous cases, and introduced the notion of a polytomous knowledge space. Building upon this work,  Sun, Li, He, et al. (2023)  further formalized the concept of a polytomous closure space.

Definition 2.3 
                            Let  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⊆ 
                                     
                                        
                                           V 
                                        
                                        
                                           Q 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                be a polytomous structure. 
                            (i)  
                                  K 
                                is defined as a  polytomous knowledge space  if, for any  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               , the  
                                  ⋓ 
                               -closure  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⋓ 
                                     L 
                                  
                                exists in  
                                  K 
                               . 
                            (ii) Similarly,  
                                  K 
                                is defined as a  polytomous closure space  if, for any  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               , the  
                                  ⋒ 
                               -closure  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⋒ 
                                     L 
                                  
                                exists in  
                                  K 
                               .

Let  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⊆ 
                                     
                                        
                                           V 
                                        
                                        
                                           Q 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                be a polytomous structure.

(i)  
                                  K 
                                is defined as a  polytomous knowledge space  if, for any  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               , the  
                                  ⋓ 
                               -closure  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⋓ 
                                     L 
                                  
                                exists in  
                                  K 
                               .

(ii) Similarly,  
                                  K 
                                is defined as a  polytomous closure space  if, for any  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               , the  
                                  ⋒ 
                               -closure  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⋒ 
                                     L 
                                  
                                exists in  
                                  K 
                               .

Given a domain  
                         Q 
                       and a finite modular lattice  
                         V 
                      , the distance between two elements  
                         K 
                       and  
                         L 
                       in  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       is defined as  
                         
                            d 
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               , 
                               L 
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            
                               
                                  ∑ 
                               
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                            δ 
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  q 
                                  ) 
                               
                               , 
                               L 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  q 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ) 
                            
                         
                      , where  
                         δ 
                       is defined in Equation  (2) . It can be easily verified that  
                         
                            d 
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               , 
                               L 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                       satisfies the properties of a distance function on  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       ( Heller, 2021 ). With these definitions established,  Sun, Li, He, et al. (2023)  introduced the concept of a well-graded family in the context of polytomous settings.

Definition 3.1 
                         A non-empty family  
                               
                                  G 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             is called  well-graded  if for any different elements  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  G 
                               
                            , there is a finite sequence in  
                               G 
                            :  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        0 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        1 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  … 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        n 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  L 
                               
                             such that 
                         (i)  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                        
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                           − 
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  1 
                               
                             for  
                               
                                  1 
                                  ≤ 
                                  i 
                                  ≤ 
                                  n 
                               
                            ; 
                         (ii)  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  n 
                               
                            . 
                         We designate a sequence  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                             ( 
                               
                                  0 
                                  ≤ 
                                  i 
                                  ≤ 
                                  n 
                               
                            ) that satisfies both conditions (i) and (ii) as a  tight path  from  
                               K 
                             to  
                               L 
                            . A sequence that satisfies only condition (i) but not necessarily (ii) is referred to as a  stepwise path  from  
                               K 
                             to  
                               L 
                            .

A non-empty family  
                               
                                  G 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             is called  well-graded  if for any different elements  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                                  ∈ 
                                  G 
                               
                            , there is a finite sequence in  
                               G 
                            :  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        0 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        1 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  … 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        n 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  L 
                               
                             such that

(i)  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                        
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                           − 
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  1 
                               
                             for  
                               
                                  1 
                                  ≤ 
                                  i 
                                  ≤ 
                                  n 
                               
                            ;

(ii)  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  n 
                               
                            .

We designate a sequence  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                             ( 
                               
                                  0 
                                  ≤ 
                                  i 
                                  ≤ 
                                  n 
                               
                            ) that satisfies both conditions (i) and (ii) as a  tight path  from  
                               K 
                             to  
                               L 
                            . A sequence that satisfies only condition (i) but not necessarily (ii) is referred to as a  stepwise path  from  
                               K 
                             to  
                               L 
                            .

It is important to note that if two mappings  
                         K 
                       and  
                         L 
                       in  
                         
                            
                               V 
                            
                            
                               Q 
                            
                         
                       have a distance of 1, it implies that  
                         K 
                       and  
                         L 
                       differ only on a single item, while maintaining the same response value for all other items. Specifically, since  
                         
                            d 
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               , 
                               L 
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            
                               
                                  ∑ 
                               
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                            δ 
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  q 
                                  ) 
                               
                               , 
                               L 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  q 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            1 
                         
                      , there exists some  
                         
                            p 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                         
                       for which  
                         
                            δ 
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  p 
                                  ) 
                               
                               , 
                               L 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  p 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            1 
                         
                      , and for all other items  
                         
                            q 
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                            ∖ 
                            
                               { 
                               p 
                               } 
                            
                         
                      ,  
                         
                            δ 
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  q 
                                  ) 
                               
                               , 
                               L 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  q 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            0 
                         
                      , meaning  
                         
                            K 
                            
                               ( 
                               q 
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            L 
                            
                               ( 
                               q 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                      . Below, we provide a simple example to explain  Definition 3.1 .

Example 3.1 
                         Let  
                               Q 
                             and  
                               V 
                             be as described in  Example 2.2 . Consider the family  
                               
                                  F 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     110 
                                     , 
                                     111 
                                     , 
                                     112 
                                     , 
                                     122 
                                     , 
                                     222 
                                     , 
                                     232 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            . It is easy to observe that the distance between any two neighboring elements in  
                               F 
                             is 1, and  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     110 
                                     , 
                                     232 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  δ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     1 
                                     , 
                                     2 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  + 
                                  δ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     1 
                                     , 
                                     3 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  + 
                                  δ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     0 
                                     , 
                                     2 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  5 
                               
                            , making  
                               F 
                             a well-graded family. The elements of  
                               F 
                             thus form a tight path from  
                               
                                  110 
                               
                             to  
                               
                                  232 
                               
                            . 
                         Now, consider another family  
                               
                                  G 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     110 
                                     , 
                                     120 
                                     , 
                                     121 
                                     , 
                                     111 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            . Although the distance between consecutive elements in  
                               G 
                             is 1, the fact that  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     110 
                                     , 
                                     111 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  1 
                               
                             implies that the elements in  
                               G 
                             only form a stepwise path from  
                               
                                  110 
                               
                             to  
                               
                                  111 
                               
                            .

Let  
                               Q 
                             and  
                               V 
                             be as described in  Example 2.2 . Consider the family  
                               
                                  F 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     110 
                                     , 
                                     111 
                                     , 
                                     112 
                                     , 
                                     122 
                                     , 
                                     222 
                                     , 
                                     232 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            . It is easy to observe that the distance between any two neighboring elements in  
                               F 
                             is 1, and  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     110 
                                     , 
                                     232 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  δ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     1 
                                     , 
                                     2 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  + 
                                  δ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     1 
                                     , 
                                     3 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  + 
                                  δ 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     0 
                                     , 
                                     2 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  5 
                               
                            , making  
                               F 
                             a well-graded family. The elements of  
                               F 
                             thus form a tight path from  
                               
                                  110 
                               
                             to  
                               
                                  232 
                               
                            .

Now, consider another family  
                               
                                  G 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     110 
                                     , 
                                     120 
                                     , 
                                     121 
                                     , 
                                     111 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            . Although the distance between consecutive elements in  
                               G 
                             is 1, the fact that  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     110 
                                     , 
                                     111 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  1 
                               
                             implies that the elements in  
                               G 
                             only form a stepwise path from  
                               
                                  110 
                               
                             to  
                               
                                  111 
                               
                            .

Remark 3.1 
                         Assuming that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                               
                             is a well-graded family, and  
                               K 
                             and  
                               L 
                             are distinct elements in  
                               K 
                            , we can make the following observations: 
                         Firstly, there exists a tight path  
                               
                                  K 
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        0 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        1 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  … 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        n 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  L 
                               
                             that satisfies the condition of well-gradedness, where  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                        
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                           − 
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  1 
                               
                             for  
                               
                                  1 
                                  ≤ 
                                  i 
                                  ≤ 
                                  n 
                               
                            , and  
                               
                                  d 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  n 
                               
                            . 
                         Now, let us consider a fixed item  
                               p 
                             in  
                               Q 
                            . The response values of the sequence  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                             
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  0 
                                  ≤ 
                                  i 
                                  ≤ 
                                  n 
                                  ) 
                               
                             at item  
                               p 
                             form the set  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              V 
                                           
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ̂ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           0 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     , 
                                     … 
                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           n 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            . By preserving the original sequence of numbers in  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           V 
                                        
                                        
                                           p 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ̂ 
                                  
                               
                             and eliminating any duplicates, we can create a new set  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 i 
                                              
                                              
                                                 0 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 i 
                                              
                                              
                                                 1 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     , 
                                     … 
                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 i 
                                              
                                              
                                                 m 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     }
