                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 q 
                                              
                                              
                                                 c 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗ 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               . This implies that there is no polytomous state  
                                  K 
                                in  
                                  K 
                                whose extension  
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                can separate  
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        b 
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        c 
                                     
                                  
                               . Therefore, the polytomous structure  
                                  K 
                                is not strongly discriminative.

In a polytomous knowledge structure  
                            K 
                         , each virtual item  
                            
                               
                                  q 
                               
                               
                                  v 
                               
                            
                          is linked to at least one polytomous state  
                            
                               K 
                               ∈ 
                               K 
                            
                         , guaranteeing that  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           q 
                                        
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                               ≠ 
                               0̸ 
                            
                         . Consequently, a minimal polytomous state exists within  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                          based on the ordering  
                            ⊑ 
                         .  Sun, Li, He, et al. (2023)  have precisely defined and denoted this concept.

Definition 4.2 
                            Let  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⊆ 
                                     
                                        
                                           V 
                                        
                                        
                                           Q 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                be a polytomous structure. For any  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           p 
                                        
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        V 
                                        ∖ 
                                        
                                           { 
                                           ⊥ 
                                           } 
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               , a state  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                                is said to be a  minimal polytomous knowledge state at  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                if it satisfies the following conditions: (i)  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     v 
                                  
                               , and (ii) for any  
                                  
                                     L 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               , if  
                                  
                                     L 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               , then  
                                  
                                     L 
                                     = 
                                     K 
                                  
                               .

The concept of the minimal polytomous knowledge state is crucial in the study of polytomous knowledge structures. While it shares similarities with atoms in KST, it exhibits distinct behaviors in certain situations, leading to potential confusion. To gain a comprehensive understanding of this concept, readers are encouraged to refer to  Stefanutti et al. (2020), Sun, Li, He, et al. (2023), Wang et al. (2023) , and  Wang et al. (2022) . These works provide valuable insights into the minimal polytomous state and its significance.

Before proceeding to  Theorem 4.2 , it is instructive to revisit the concept of well-gradedness in dichotomous KST, as discussed in Section  2.1 . In this context, well-gradedness is characterized by the unique correspondence of each atom to a single item, meaning that for every atom in a dichotomous knowledge space  
                            K 
                         , there exists exactly one item that it is associated with. This equivalence, however, may not directly translate to polytomous settings, as we will see in the forthcoming theorem and example.

Theorem 4.2 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               be a well-graded polytomous knowledge space. If  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               belongs to  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        M 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               , then it is a minimal polytomous knowledge state at only one virtual item  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               . In other words, the family  
                               
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        
                                           M 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     ∣ 
                                     
                                        
                                           p 
                                        
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        V 
                                        ∖ 
                                        
                                           { 
                                           ⊥ 
                                           } 
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     } 
                                  
                                
                               forms a partition of  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        M 
                                     
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               .

Let  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               be a well-graded polytomous knowledge space. If  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               belongs to  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        M 
                                     
                                     
                                        K

                                        V

                                is well-graded, there exists a tight path from  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     ×

                                        ⊥

                               , passing through

                                     ×

                                     K

                                           − 
                                           1

                                     K

                                        K

                                     1

                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     ≤ 
                                     K 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               ,  
                                  
                                     δ

                                              −

                                           p

                                        K

                                           p

                                     1

                                     Q

                                        { 
                                        p

                            Suppose that

The following example intends to demonstrate merely satisfying the condition where the family  
                            
                               { 
                               
                                  
                                     M

                                           v

                               }

                                  K 
                               
                            
                         , as stated in  Theorem 4.2 , does not necessarily guarantee that the polytomous knowledge space  
                            K 
                          has the well-gradedness property.

Example 4.2 
                            Consider  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        p 
                                        , 
                                        q 
                                        }

                                        }

                                  K

                                           =

                                              }

                                              30 
                                              , 
                                              31 
                                              ,

                                              , 
                                              33 
                                              }

                                                 }

                                     10

                                     2 
                                  
                               . This indicates that

Consider  
                                  
                                     Q

                                           =

                                              }

                                              ,

                                              , 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    M 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          p 
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          2 
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 21 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              , 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    M 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          p 
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          3 
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 30 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              ; 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    M 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          q 
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          1 
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 01 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              , 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    M 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          q 
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          2 
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 02 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              , 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    M 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          q 
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          3 
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 03 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              . 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                Therefore, each minimal polytomous state corresponds to exactly one virtual item. However, it is observed that  
                                  
                                     10 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     30 
                                  
                                and they are adjacent in  
                                  K 
                               , but  
                                  
                                     d 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        10 
                                        , 
                                        30 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     2 
                                  
                               . This indicates that  
                                  K 
                                does not possess the well-gradedness property, despite satisfying conditions of  Theorem 4.2 .

A remarkable contribution by  Heller (2021)  is the generalization of the precedence relation in KST to the polytomous case. This generalization introduces a binary relation for pairs of virtual items  
                            
                               
                                  p 
                               
                               
                                  v 
                               
                            
                          and  
                            
                               
                                  q 
                               
                               
                                  w 
                               
                            
                         , denoted as  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     w 
                                  
                               
                            
                         . It is interpreted as follows: if an individual’s response to item  
                            q 
                          is at least  
                            w 
                         , it can be inferred that their response to item  
                            p 
                          will be at least  
                            v 
                         . Heller further formalized a  precedence relation  
                         
                            ≼ 
                          on  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               V 
                            
                          that satisfies the following four specific conditions:

(i)  
                            ≼ 
                          is a quasi-ordering relation.

(ii) For any  
                            
                               p 
                               ∈ 
                               Q 
                            
                          and  
                            
                               v 
                               , 
                               w 
                               ∈ 
                               V 
                            
                         ,  
                            
                               v 
                               ≤ 
                               w 
                            
                          iff  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     w 
                                  
                               
                            
                         .

(iii) For any  
                            
                               p 
                               , 
                               q 
                               ∈ 
                               Q 
                            
                          and  
                            
                               u 
                               , 
                               v 
                               , 
                               w 
                               ∈ 
                               V 
                            
                         , if  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     u 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     w 
                                  
                               
                            
                          and  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     w 
                                  
                               
                            
                         , then  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     u 
                                     ∨ 
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     w 
                                  
                               
                            
                         .

(iv) For any  
                            
                               p 
                               , 
                               q 
                               ∈ 
                               Q 
                            
                          and  
                            
                               v 
                               ∈ 
                               V 
                            
                         ,  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     ⊥ 
                                  
                               
                            
                          iff  
                            
                               v 
                               = 
                               ⊥ 
                            
                         .

Heller’s work demonstrates a one-to-one correspondence between the precedence relations  
                            ≼ 
                          on  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               V 
                            
                          and specific polytomous structures  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                            
                          on  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                         . The polytomous structure  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                            
                          is defined as follows:  
                            
                               (17) 
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ≼ 
                                        
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        K 
                                        ∈ 
                                        
                                           
                                              V 
                                           
                                           
                                              Q 
                                           
                                        
                                        ∣ 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                           ≼ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 q 
                                              
                                              
                                                 w 
                                              
                                           
                                           and 
                                           w 
                                           ≤ 
                                           K 
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              q 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        imply 
                                        v 
                                        ≤ 
                                        K 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           p 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        } 
                                     
                                     . 
                                  
                               
                            
                         By combining the methodologies of  Schrepp (1997)  and  Stefanutti et al. (2020) , Heller introduces a novel framework that reveals a specific precedence relation and its corresponding polytomous knowledge structure as follows:

Definition 4.3 
                            Given a quasi-order  
                                  
                                     
                                        ≺ 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                on  
                                  Q 
                               , a precedence relation  
                                  ≼ 
                                on  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                                can be defined as:  
                                  
                                     (18) 
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 p 
                                              
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                           ≼ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 q 
                                              
                                              
                                                 w 
                                              
                                           
                                           iff 
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          v 
                                                          = 
                                                          ⊥ 
                                                          , 
                                                          or 
                                                          
                                                       
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          ⊥ 
                                                          ≠ 
                                                          v 
                                                          ≤ 
                                                          w 
                                                          and 
                                                          p 
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                ≺ 
                                                             
                                                             
                                                                Q 
                                                             
                                                          
                                                          q 
                                                          
                                                       
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               for any  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           p 
                                        
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                     , 
                                     
                                        
                                           q 
                                        
                                        
                                           w 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∈ 
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V 
                                  
                               . A precedence relation  
                                  ≼ 
                                that satisfies this condition for a given quasi-order  
                                  
                                     
                                        ≺ 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                on  
                                  Q 
                                is referred to as  factorial . Moreover, a polytomous structure  
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                     
                                  
                                that satisfies  (17)  when the precedence relation  
                                  ≼ 
                                is factorial is called a  factorial polytomous knowledge structure .

Given a quasi-order  
                                  
                                     
                                        ≺ 
                                     
                                     
                                        Q 
                                     
                                  
                                on  
                                  Q

                                  ≼

                                     Q

Example 4.3

                                     ( 
                                     V 
                                     , 
                                     ≤ 
                                     )

                                     b 
                                     ≤ 
                                     d

                                     ≤ 
                                     c

                                     d

                                  p

                                     q

                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                for any  
                                  
                                     v 
                                     ∈ 
                                     V

                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ≼

                                     K

                                        p 
                                        )

                                     K 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        q 
                                        )

                                        {

                                        b

(2020) , offers a succinct expression for the minimal polytomous states within the factorial polytomous structure. Moreover, it proves that the factorial polytomous structure is closed under the  
                            ⋒ 
                          and  
                            ⋓ 
                          operations.

Lemma 4.2 
                            
                               Consider the factorial polytomous structure  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               generated by  
                               (17)

                                           p 
                                        
                                     
                                     =

                                        r

                                        r 
                                        
                                           
                                              ≺

                                        p 
                                        }

                                     v 
                                     ≠ 
                                     ⊥ 
                                  
                               
                               . Then  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        M 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                
                               can be expressed as follows:  
                               
                                  
                                     (19) 
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 M 
                                              
                                              
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       p

                                              r

                                                          v 
                                                          , 
                                                          
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          if 
                                                          r 
                                                          ∈ 
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                Q 
                                                             
                                                             
                                                                p 
                                                             
                                                          
                                                          ; 
                                                       
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          ⊥ 
                                                          ,

                                                          r 
                                                          ∈ 
                                                          Q 
                                                          ∖ 
                                                          
                                                             
                                                                Q 
                                                             
                                                             
                                                                p 
                                                             
                                                          
                                                          .

                                        ≼

(iii)  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               is both a polytomous knowledge space and a polytomous closure space.

By combining  Theorem 4.1 (i), the following lemma establishes a direct equivalence between the discriminative nature of the factorial polytomous structure and its strong discriminative property. Additionally, through the analysis of the precedence relation among virtual items, valuable insights can be gained regarding their minimal polytomous states. These insights will be utilized in the subsequent proof process of the theorem.

Proof

                                           }

                                such that for any polytomous state  
                                  K 
                                in

                                                 w 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗

                            As the polytomous structure  
                                  K 
                                is factorial, it implies the existence of a factorial precedence relation  
                                  ≼ 
                                on  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                     × 
                                     V

                                           w

                                           ∗

                                                 w 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ∗

                                                 v 
                                              
                                           
                                           .

                                under the specified conditions, then  Lemma 4.2  (ii) implies that  
                                  
                                     s 
                                     
                                        
                                           ≺ 
                                        
                                        
                                           Q 
                                        
                                     
                                     r 
                                  
                               . Since  
                                  
                                     r 
                                     
                                        
                                           ≺ 
                                        
                                        
                                           Q 
                                        
                                     
                                     q 
                                  
                               , it follows that  
                                  
                                     s 
                                     
                                        
                                           ≺ 
                                        
                                        
                                           Q 
                                        
                                     
                                     q 
                                  
                               . However, this contradicts the fact that  
                                  s 
                                does not belong to  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 Q 
                                              
                                              
                                                 1 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                  
                               . Therefore, there cannot exist such an  
                                  s 
                               , and we conclude that  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           M 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 r 
                                              
                                              
                                                 u 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        s 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     ⊥ 
                                  
                                for all  
                                  
                                     s 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           Q 
                                        
                                        
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     ∖ 
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    Q 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    1 
                                                 
                                              
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           q 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               . □

In the following discussion, we will explore the fact that adjacent states in a polytomous closure space (or polytomous knowledge space) must be comparable with respect to the partial order  
                            ⊑ 
                         .

Lemma 4.4 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               be a polytomous closure space or polytomous knowledge space, and let  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               and  
                               
                                  L 
                                
                               be two adjacent polytomous states in  
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               . Then either  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     L 
                                  
                                
                               or  
                               
                                  
                                     L 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               
                               .

Let  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               be a polytomous closure space or polytomous knowledge space, and let  
                               
                                  K 
                                
                               and  
                               
                                  L 
                                
                               be two adjacent polytomous states in  
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               . Then either  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     L 
                                  
                                
                               or  
                               
                                  
                                     L 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               
                               .

Proof 
                            Assuming that polytomous states  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                                are adjacent in the polytomous closure space  
                                  K 
                               , we have  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⋒ 
                                     L 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               . If  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                                are incomparable in  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     ⊑ 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               , it implies that  
                                  
                                     
                                        { 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        , 
                                        K 
                                        ⋒ 
                                        L 
                                        } 
                                     
                                     ⊆ 
                                     
                                        [ 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        ] 
                                     
                                  
                               . However, this contradicts the fact that  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                                are adjacent, as the interval  
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                should not contain additional elements beyond  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                               . By employing similar reasoning, we can conclude that adjacent states in a polytomous knowledge space must also be comparable. □

Assuming that polytomous states  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                                are adjacent in the polytomous closure space  
                                  K 
                               , we have  
                                  
                                     K 
                                     ⋒ 
                                     L 
                                     ∈ 
                                     K 
                                  
                               . If  
                                  K

                                  L

                                     K

                                        L

                                        L 
                                        } 
                                     
                                     ⊆ 
                                     
                                        [ 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        ] 
                                     
                                  
                               . However, this contradicts the fact that  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                                are adjacent, as the interval  
                                  
                                     [ 
                                     K 
                                     , 
                                     L 
                                     ] 
                                  
                                should not contain additional elements beyond  
                                  K 
                                and  
                                  L 
                               . By employing similar reasoning, we can conclude that adjacent states in a polytomous knowledge space must also be comparable. □

With these preliminary definitions and lemmas established, we are now prepared to state the main theorem of this subsection.

Theorem 4.3 
                            
                               If the factorial polytomous knowledge structure  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               is (strongly) discriminative, then  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               is well-graded.

                                is not valid. Thus, we can conclude that

                                        ≼

To summarize, we have shown that both Case I and Case II lead to contradictions, which means that the assumption  
                                  
                                     d 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        K 
                                        , 
                                        L 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     ≥ 
                                     2 
                                  
                                is not valid. Thus, we can conclude that  
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ≼ 
                                     
                                  
                                is well-graded. □

The  aligned precedence relation  
                         
                            ≼ 
                          is defined such that for any  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               , 
                               
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     w 
                                  
                               
                               ∈ 
                               Q 
                               × 
                               V 
                            
                         , if  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     v 
                                  
                               
                               ≼ 
                               
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     w 
                                  
                               
                            
                         , then  
                            
                               v 
                               ≤ 
                               w 
                            
                         . The alignment property ensures that every set  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               
                                  { 
                                  v 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         , where  
                            v 
                          belongs to  
                            V 
                         , is included in the polytomous structure  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ≼ 
                               
                            
                          defined by  (17)  ( Heller, 2021 ). However, it is important to note that modifying the factorial polytomous structure described in  Theorem 4.3  to the polytomous structure  
                            K 
                          generated by the aligned precedence relation  
                            ≼ 
                          may not necessarily guarantee the well-gradedness property.

Example 4.4

                                  V

                               The polytomous structure

In Section  2.1 , we presented several important findings regarding the well-graded properties of classic KST. These findings provide a basis for extending the conclusions to polytomous KST. However, it is essential to acknowledge that these extensions are not straightforward due to the increased complexity of polytomous KST. Unlike classic KST, where the response level to an item is binary (fully correct or fully incorrect), polytomous KST may involve a response scale as general as a modular lattice, making the determination of response levels more intricate.

To ensure the validity of metrics and obtain reliable measurements in polytomous KST, it is crucial to consider modular lattices.  Example 2.1  illustrates that a general graded lattice may not guarantee the metric property of  
                         δ 
                       defined in Equation  (2) . Additionally, it cannot ensure that the distance function  
                         d 
                       defined in  Definition 3.1  satisfies the properties of a metric. Thus, incorporating modular lattices becomes crucial in polytomous KST to establish valid metrics and reliable measurements.

Once we establish that  
                         d 
                       is a metric, we delve into a comprehensive analysis of  Definition 3.1 . Determining the response level for an item  
                         q 
                       in the polytomous case is significantly more complex than in the dichotomous case. As a result, the definition of well-gradedness in polytomous KST becomes more intricate compared to classical KST. However, it is worth noting that the distance between  
                         K 
                       and  
                         L 
                       must adhere to the relationship of  
                         
                            d 
                            
                               ( 
                               K 
                               , 
                               L 
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            n 
                         
                      , where  
                         n 
                       represents the number of elements in the tight path  
                         
                            K 
                            = 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  0 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  1 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  2 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            … 
                            , 
                            L 
                            = 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  n 
                               
                            
                         
                      . This requirement imposes specific patterns on each component of the family  
                         
                            F 
                            =

                                     }

                                  0 
                               
                               
                                  n

                            K 
                            
                               ( 
                               q

                         V

                               K
